design_requirements: Use fewer but independently sourced datasets with explicit source/version/license records.
Represent task variants under their source dataset instead of counting them as independent datasets.
Use the same alpha grid, seed count, split policy, learner/config surface, and conformal wrappers within each paired task.
Compare conformal wrappers within exact learner/config/split cells rather than averaging across mismatched model grids.
Do not recount a fixed CQR backend across unrelated outer model-grid rows.
Fit imputation, scaling, capping, feature selection, and dimensionality reduction inside each CV+/jackknife fold.
Separate IID, grouped, temporal, spatial, and shift regimes before aggregation.
Freeze the primary utility rule, exclusion rules, and pathology-display policy before compute starts.
Report source-dataset-clustered bootstrap or hierarchical meta-analysis rather than row-level pseudo-inference.
Reserve untouched post-protocol datasets for external validation.
Publish planned, attempted, completed, failed, skipped, and publication-scoped accounting matrices.
Publish enough run-level ledger, split hash, environment lock, and aggregate cube material to audit the synthesis.
interpretation_scope: Benchmark v2 is a forward protocol. It does not revise the Atlas v0 retrospective results until new, separately versioned runs are completed.

primary_estimand.primary_utility_rule: pre-specified coverage-lower-bound gate followed by interval score, reported with sensitivity to absolute coverage error and width

purpose: Define the next balanced, paired, leakage-safe regression conformal prediction benchmark before opening new result generation.
